questions.q: What does the radio code **10-4** commonly mean?
In the NATO phonetic alphabet, which word stands for the letter **P**?
questions.fact: The NATO alphabet runs Alfa, Bravo, Charlie… Papa is P.
An APB broadcasts a suspect or vehicle description to all units.

questions.q: A dispatcher asking your **10-20** wants to know what?

questions.fact: SWAT teams handle high-risk operations beyond regular patrol capability.

questions.q: Which letter does **Foxtrot** represent in the phonetic alphabet?
questions.fact: Foxtrot is F — as in "unit Foxtrot-1".
K-9 is a play on "canine" — dogs trained for detection and apprehension.
A BOLO alert asks units to watch for a person or vehicle.

questions.q: When an officer says **"copy"** on the radio, they mean…
questions.fact: "Copy" acknowledges the transmission was received and understood.
Short for perpetrator — the person who committed the offense.
